LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FAHAD GHANI'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA'S POLICY OF LIBERALLY ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS REQUIRES THAT THIS MOTION BE GRANTED. GATEWAY INSURANCE COMPANY'S CONTENTION THAT MR. GHANI WILL NOT BE ABLE TO ESTABLISH LIABILITY AGAINST MR. ALSHURAIDAH IS NOT A BASIS TO DENY LEAVE TO AMEND. IN THE EVENT THAT MR. ALSHURAIDAH APPEARS IN THIS CASE, HE CAN FILE ANY APPROPRIATE MOTION CHALLENGING LIABILITY, INCLUDING A MOTION PER CCP 128.7. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT MUST BE FILED NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 21, 2018. TRIAL DATE OF APRIL 15, 2019 IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 28, 2019 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM; (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Summons And First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Westley Esq., Attorney at Law, Telephone 415-362-2817, appearing for Plaintiff;
Lucy K. Galek, Esq.,Heath & Yuen, Telephone 415-373-3957, appearing for Plaintiff-in-Intervention, Gateway Insurance Company;

Ruling - AR G U E D. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Fahad Ghani's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted. California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings requires that this motion be granted. Gateway Insurance Company's contention that Mr. Ghani will not be able to establish liability against Mr. Alshuraidah is not a basis to deny leave to amend. In the event that Mr. Alshuraidah appears in this case, he can file any appropriate motion challenging liability, including a motion per CCP 128.7. The first amended complaint must be filed no later than December 21, 2018. Trial date of April 15, 2019 is continued to October 28, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 206. Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; (302/HEK)

PLAINTIFF FAHAD GHANI's Motion To Compel Initial Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demand For Production, And Requests For Admission; To Compel Attendance At Deposition; And For A Monetary Sanction.

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion To Strike Defendants Answer
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FAHAD GHANI's Motion To Strike Defendant's Answer.

Plaintiff Fahad Ghani's motion to strike the answer filed by defendant Uber Taxi Cab LLC is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

ATTORNEYS HEATH & YUEN, APC'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL OF RECORD FOR DEFENDANT UBER TAXI CAB LLC DBA GOLD STAR TAXI CAB is GRANTED. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org (L&M302)

Motion To Intervene
Off Calendar Jan-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Proposed Intervenor GATEWAY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Intervene is GRANTED. Gateway may file its proposed complaint in intervention no later than January 19, 2018. Per Reliance Insurance Co. v. Superior Court (2000) 84 Cal. App. 4th 383 and cases following it, Gateway is entitled to intervene in this case to protect its own interests due to the suspended status of its insured. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org (L&M302)
